title: Delayed fluorescence from inverted singlet and triplet excited states

type2_summary: No clear raw/unprocessed instrument files were provided. The dataset does not include proprietary instrument exports, scan files, or other obvious raw measurement outputs.

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports a heptazine-based organic emitter, HzTFEX2, that experimentally shows an inverted singlet–triplet gap (ΔEST = −11 ± 2 meV), producing delayed fluorescence from a singlet state below the triplet state. The authors combine quantum-chemical screening, synthesis, photophysical spectroscopy, and OLED fabrication to show fast sub-microsecond delayed fluorescence and a peak external quantum efficiency of 17% in a blue OLED.
measurement_types: time-correlated single-photon counting transient PL decay

figures.description: Schematic of singlet and triplet excited-state splitting by exchange interaction and inversion by double-excitation effects.
General molecular design of heptazine analogues with two substituent positions R1 and R2; 186 substituents generate 34,596 candidates.
Screening statistics showing number of molecules as a function of ΔEST and oscillator strength f from TDDFT.
S1–S0 energy gap versus ΔEST and oscillator strength f, color-coded by fluorescence color.
Molecular structures of lead candidates HzTFEX2 and HzPipX2 with EOM-CCSD-calculated ΔEST values.
Dominant natural transition orbitals for S1 and T1 of HzTFEX2.
Dominant natural transition orbitals for S1 and T1 of HzPipX2.
Steady-state absorption and photoluminescence spectra of HzTFEX2 and HzPipX2 in deaerated toluene.
Temperature-dependent transient PL decays of HzTFEX2.
Temperature-dependent transient PL decays of HzPipX2.
Temperature dependence of delayed fluorescence time constants τDF for both molecules with exponential fits versus inverse temperature.
Excited-state kinetic scheme and fitted rate constants for HzTFEX2.
Excited-state kinetic scheme and fitted rate constants for HzPipX2.
OLED current density–voltage–luminance characteristics and inset EL spectra for HzTFEX2 device.
External quantum efficiency versus luminance for the OLED and inset viewing-angle luminance dependence.
Transient electroluminescence decays of OLEDs using HzTFEX2, 4CzIPN, and MADN.
Transient absorption map of HzTFEX2, integrated transient absorption spectra in deaerated and aerated toluene, and decays monitored at 700 nm and 1600 nm.
figures.data_type: time-resolved spectroscopy map and decay curves

figures.description: Fits of S1 population dynamics to transient PL decay data for HzTFEX2 and HzPipX2, with simulated T1 populations.
Temperature dependence of kISC and kRISC for both molecules with Arrhenius fits and a schematic of activation energies.
Photophysical properties of HzTFEX2 in solid-state host matrix: PL spectra, transient PL decays, temperature dependence of τDF, and kISC/kRISC.
Tabulated photophysical parameters for HzTFEX2 and HzPipX2, including λPL, ΦPL, τPF, τDF, kr, knr, kISC, kRISC, activation energies, and ΔEST.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements include UV–vis absorption spectra, steady-state PL spectra, absolute PL quantum yield measurements, transient absorption traces/maps, transient PL decay traces at multiple temperatures, ionization potential measurements by ultraviolet photoelectron yield spectroscopy, OLED EL spectra, current density–voltage–luminance data, viewing-angle luminance data, and transient EL decay traces. The paper names the instruments used but does not provide file formats or raw file names in the text.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states: 'The data underlying this article are available at https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.20058977.' It also notes that online content includes source data, extended data, supplementary information, and a Nature Research Reporting Summary.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: The text indicates that underlying data are deposited on Figshare, but it does not specify raw file formats or a raw-data directory structure. Raw experimental data likely correspond to spectroscopy traces, decay curves, and device characterization measurements collected with the listed instruments.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include fitted kinetic parameters (kr, knr, kISC, kRISC), activation energies, ΔEST values, photophysical summary values in Extended Data Table 1, and derived quantities such as oscillator strengths, energy gaps, and screening statistics from TDDFT/EOM-CCSD/ADC(2)/CASPT2 calculations.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Yes. The paper explicitly mentions 'source data' in the online content statement, implying figure-level source data are provided. Extended Data figures and tables are also part of the article package, and the Figshare deposit is stated to contain the underlying data.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: Analysis scripts are mentioned only indirectly: transient PL decay fitting used 'scipy.integrate.odeint' and 'scipy.optimize.curve_fit' in Python 3.7. No standalone scripts or notebooks are explicitly stated as deposited.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset mixes experimental measurements, derived kinetic parameters, and theoretical/computational screening outputs. The computational dataset is large and enumerative: 34,596 heptazine candidates screened by TDDFT, with a smaller subset evaluated by higher-level methods. The paper distinguishes between raw spectral/decay measurements and processed quantities extracted by fitting and Arrhenius analysis. Figure-specific source data are likely present, but the text does not enumerate file names or exact repository contents.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The Methods section describes direct measurements of absorption, PL, transient absorption, transient PL, photoelectron yield, and OLED EL/J-V-L characteristics, and the Data availability section says the underlying data are available on Figshare.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The online content statement explicitly includes 'source data'; Extended Data Table 1 and multiple fitted plots indicate processed figure-ready data are part of the article package.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “The data underlying this article are available at https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.20058977.”; “Online content ... source data, extended data, supplementary information ... are available ...”; “The details of the synthesis and characterization are given in the Supplementary Information.”
